items.title: Monitoring started: Franklin County Probate Court

page_lines: Important Notice: Wednesday is a designated cou
rt day and services may be limited or unavailable during normal business hours.
Thursdays and Fridays, 9:00 am to 3:00 pm
See below for more detailed information about the passport application process or click
Please remember to bring the following to your passport appointment:
Do not sign the form before your appointment as it must be signed in front of a Passport Agent.
Please bring a state-issued birth certificate or previous U.S. passport.
Bring a physical photo ID. (Typically a valid driver’s license)
If your photo ID is from a different state than the state in which you are applying, bring a second photo ID.
Passport fees can be paid by check or money order payable to “U.S. Department of State”
Processing fees can be paid by check to “Probate Court” or by cash.
**For couples or families, each passport application must have a separate check or money order.
You must provide a recent photo with your application. We can take and print a photo for you if you do not already have one. ($15.00 fee)
US Department of State – passport information
